meta.description: Kent & Medway Winter Vulnerability Index — Assistiv Systems
meta.refresh_type: monthly — runs 12th of each month via hes_shmi_gp_monthly.yml

sources.fep: kent-fep-data.json (daily refresh — Assistiv Systems FEP pipeline)
sources.fingertips: NHS Fingertips/OHID — COPD admissions, flu vaccination, falls
sources.evidence: NICE NG147 (falls), NICE NG191 (frailty), BGS Frailty Toolkit 2024, ONS excess winter deaths, NHS Digital HES, NHS England UEC data

meta.licence: NHS data published under Open Government Licence v3.0

tiers.critical: WVI ≥ 70 — highest winter decompensation risk, accelerated deployment recommended
tiers.high: WVI 55–69 — elevated risk, September–October deployment essential
tiers.elevated: WVI 40–54 — above-average risk, proactive planning will yield measurable impact
tiers.managed: WVI < 40 — manageable risk with standard winter planning

meta.commissioner_note: WVI is a forward-looking planning tool, not a clinical assessment. It connects existing NHS open data — FEP scores, corridor care, GP registers, Fingertips outcomes — into a single composite that indicates where preventative deployment will have the greatest impact on January admission pressure. Decisions made in August determine what can be deployed by October.

deployment_evidence.medication_review: NICE NG191 — 4 weeks lead time for structured review to optimise medication pre-winter; reduces emergency admissions and medication non-adherence over festive period.
deployment_evidence.falls_prevention: NICE NG147 — Otago exercise programme requires 6–8 weeks before measurable falls reduction; home hazard assessment 4–6 weeks; deploy by October for January impact.
deployment_evidence.respiratory_outreach: UKHSA guidance — flu vaccination ideally complete by end of October; COPD review identifies patients at highest respiratory risk before winter.
deployment_evidence.social_connection: BGS Frailty Toolkit 2024 — social prescribing requires 6–8 weeks to meaningful engagement; November deployment targets Christmas isolation peak.

Maidstone.winter_narrative: Maidstone is one of Kent's higher-risk districts for winter frailty decompensation, with 16,700 adults aged 75 and over on GP registers and a Frailty Emergence Probability of 60 — above the England average. The serving trust (Maidstone & Tunbridge Wells) is already managing an average of 21 corridor care patients per day in May 2026, leaving limited buffer before the winter surge that historically drives falls admissions 27% above the annual average in January. A September–October deployment of medication review and falls prevention outreach in Maidstone is the evidence-supported window to have measurable impact on January admission pressure.
Maidstone.trust_name: Maidstone and Tunbridge Wells NHS Trust

Thanet.winter_narrative: Thanet is an elevated-risk district requiring targeted winter planning, with 18,200 adults aged 75 and over on GP registers and a Frailty Emergence Probability of 61 — above the England average. The serving trust (East Kent Hospitals) is already managing an average of 1 corridor care patients per day in May 2026, leaving limited buffer before the winter surge that historically drives falls admissions 27% above the annual average in January. A September–October deployment of medication review and falls prevention outreach in Thanet is the evidence-supported window to have measurable impact on January admission pressure.

deployment_planning_calendar.rationale: NICE evidence on intervention lead times requires September–October deployment to achieve measurable impact by the January–February admission peak. Commissioner decisions must therefore be made by August 2026.
